Description (from Project):
Smart Account Onboarding is an open-source reference implementation and onboarding standard that removes the primary adoption friction for Soroban smart accounts: funding a C-address without forcing end-users to first create and manage a traditional G-address.

The project delivers:

-   a G→C seamless funding bridge (supporting inbound funding from existing G-addresses, CEX withdrawals, and optional off-ramp rails), making the “G-address step” transparent or unnecessary for the user,  
    
-   a production-grade reference C-address wallet (web + mobile) with token holdings and transfer history at parity with Freighter,  
    
-   a reusable Onboarding Kit (UI + SDK) that wallet providers can embed to standardize the “create C-address → fund fees → add assets → bridge funds” journey across the ecosystem.  
    

Implementation aligns with the OpenZeppelin Smart Account standard, using its smart-account primitives (policies/signers/verifiers) to support modern auth patterns and extensibility.
Products & Services:
This submission delivers an end-to-end, open-source G→C onboarding + passkey account abstraction stack for Soroban, packaged as reusable ecosystem primitives: a 3-contract Smart Account suite, a reference Passkey Wallet Web App, and an Onboarding SDK that any wallet or dApp can embed.

####   
I- Soroban Smart Account Contract Suite (deterministic C-address lifecycle + passkeys)

Three Soroban contracts that cover the full lifecycle of C-address creation and passkey-based authentication, optimized for low per-user deployment cost and predictable onboarding flows.

1) Factory (Deterministic Deployment)

-   Deterministically deploys a user Smart Account via Stellar/Soroban deterministic deployment (deployer_with_address).
    
-   get_c_address(funder, …) allows wallets/dApps to pre-compute the future C-address before deployment, enabling pre-funding (onramps, escrow rails, “fund-first” onboarding).
    
-   create_account(funder, credential_pubkey, …) deploys + initializes the Smart Account and registers the user’s passkey as the initial signer, while lazy-deploying a shared component (the WebAuthn verifier) when required.
    

=> Outcome: one-call provisioning that turns G→C migration into a simple, deterministic onboarding primitive for the ecosystem.

  
1. Smart Account (Account Abstraction + On-chain Policy)

-   Implements OpenZeppelin smart-account interfaces/traits (e.g., CustomAccountInterface, SmartAccount, ExecutionEntryPoint).
    
-   Uses Stellar/Soroban’s native __check_auth hook to enforce:
    
    -   Passkey-authenticated execution (WebAuthn-backed signatures).
        
    -   Scoped session keys via OZ context rules (least-privilege dApp access: allowlists, spend limits, expirations).
        
    -   On-chain policy enforcement so trust boundaries remain verifiable and auditable.
        

  
=> Outcome: modern wallet UX (passkeys + sessions) with security guarantees enforced on-chain.

3) WebAuthn Verifier (Shared P-256 Verifier)

-   Stateless secp256r1 / P-256 verifier implementing OpenZeppelin’s Verifier trait.
    
-   Deployed once and reused across all Smart Accounts to keep per-account deployment overhead low.
    

=> Outcome: passkeys become a first-class signing method for Soroban Smart Accounts without per-user verifier deployments.

-  

#### II- Passkey Wallet Web App (reference implementation + integration target)

A standalone, browser-based wallet that manages Soroban Smart Accounts with passkeys and provides a clean, auditable UX for G→C onboarding and day-to-day dApp interactions.

Key capabilities:

-   Passkey onboarding: create/register passkeys via the WebAuthn browser API; sign Soroban authorization entries with no seed phrase.
    
-   Guided G→C onboarding flow: generate an ephemeral G-address, detect funding, create passkey, deploy the C-address Smart Account — with atomic on-chain execution wherever applicable.
    
-   dApp request routing (stable callback URL): a persistent wallet URL endpoint that dApps can redirect to with a signing request, then redirect back with signed authorization entries (deep-link UX).
    
-   Large request + multi-sig friendliness: optional store-and-forward via refractor.space when payloads don’t fit in URLs and to simplify multi-step / multi-signer orchestration.
    
-   Session key management UI: create/revoke scoped session keys aligned with on-chain OZ context rules.
    
-   Gas abstraction: integrates Stellar Development Foundation’s OpenZeppelin Relayer / Channels Service so users can transact without holding XLM for fees.
    
-   Wallet parity basics: token balances + transfer history for the Smart Account, comparable to existing Stellar wallets.
    

=> Outcome: proves end-to-end feasibility, provides a UX baseline, and serves as a reference client for ecosystem integrators.

-  

#### III- Onboarding SDK (embed G→C + passkeys into any wallet or dApp)

An open-source JavaScript/TypeScript SDK that lets third-party wallets and dApps adopt C-address onboarding and Smart Account interactions without re-implementing the flow.

Includes:

-   Pre-built UI components for G→C migration (funding step, passkey creation, deployment confirmation).
    
-   Transaction builders + helpers for Factory interactions, Smart Account operations, session key creation, and policy configuration.
    
-   Transport adapters for deep-links and refractor.space (oversized payload handling + smoother multi-party signing).
    
-   Documentation + examples showing how to add C-address support to an existing wallet and how dApps can request signatures safely.
    

=> Outcome: turns Smart Accounts + passkey onboarding into a reusable ecosystem primitive, accelerating adoption across wallets, dApps, and onboarding rails.
Traction Evidence:
We built a working PoC of the wallet web app at https://mysoroban.xzy/dapp. It demonstrates the full onboarding workflow (G→C) and the core dApp interaction pattern where the “wallet” is a subdomain per smart-account contract, solving the WebAuthn constraint that passkeys are scoped to origins/domains (previously implying “one passkey per dApp”).

Concretely: a dApp redirects the user to their account subdomain with the hash of the auth entry in the URL; the wallet uses the passkey to sign the authorization entry and redirects back. Next, we’ll pass the full transaction payload, submit it via the OpenZeppelin relayer (fee abstraction), and return execution results to the dApp. For payloads too large for URLs (and smoother multisig flows), we’ll store unsigned transactions on refractor.space by Stellar Expert.

Team credibility: The Aha Company has a strong delivery record, including major contributions to the Stellar CLI, and institutional work such as bringing Société Générale-Forge’s EURCV onto Stellar rails. We’ve also shipped multiple Stellar Community Fund–funded deliverables (e.g., Loam, Moonlight, Scaffold Stellar), and we actively support ecosystem governance and developer education (mostly through hackathons).

Description (from Project): A Soroban-First Block Explorer Powered by flowctl
Products & Services:
OBSRVR Prism extends our existing open-source in development block explorer — the Obsrvr Intelligence Console (Terminal) — with full Soroban-first capabilities. Terminal already provides a contract-centric explorer with tiered hot/cold storage and real-time WebSocket streaming. Prism adds the missing Soroban display layer: human-readable transactions, CAP-67 event support, and complete ledger history from genesis.

  

Existing Foundation — What's Already Built (Stellar Integration):

Terminal is a block explorer running on Obsrvr Lake with tiered storage — PostgreSQL for recent data and S3 Parquet/DuckDB for full history.

Full History (Stellar Integration): Ingests all 61M+ Stellar ledgers using flowctl (open-source) and our gRPC-based TTP processor pipeline, streaming from Stellar archives into PostgreSQL. No gaps in Soroban-era data.

Human-Readable Soroban (Impact): Implements SEP-41 and SEP-50 to transform opaque XDR into plain language — "Swapped 100 USDC for 1,250 XLM via Soroswap." Makes DeFi, collectibles, and smart contract activity accessible to all users.

CAP-67 Event Stream (Stellar Integration): Full unified event stream with swap detection heuristics for major DEXes (Soroswap, Aquarius, Phoenix). Filterable by contract, function call, event type, and token.

Classic + Soroban Parity (Impact): Unified interface for classic operations, SAC tokens, SEP-39, and Soroban smart contracts — complete coverage of the Stellar ecosystem in one explorer.

Obsrvr Lake Backend (Stellar Integration): Bronze layer (immutable raw XDR in object storage) + Silver layer (transformed and filterd) backed by Stellar Galexie archives. Already running on testnet. Long-term sustainability guaranteed — explorer infrastructure is maintained as part of Obsrvr's core platform, not an isolated grant project.

Fully Open Source: Pipeline, processors, API, and frontend. Some core components already public on GitHub.
Traction Evidence:
Open-Source Tooling (Live on GitHub):

• nebu v0.3.8 — Stellar ledger processor with 10 built-in processors (contract-events, token-transfer, usdc-filter, contract-invocation, etc.). https://github.com/withObsrvr/nebu

• Flowctl based TTP Processor Pipeline — gRPC microservices for streaming Stellar events to PostgreSQL. Built on SDF's TTP-as-a-Service architecture concept for processors (stellar/go#5637). https://github.com/withObsrvr/ttp-processor-demo

• Terminal (Obsrvr Intelligence Console) — Contract-centric block explorer and developer terminal for Stellar/Soroban. Features tiered storage (Hot PostgreSQL + Cold S3 Parquet/DuckDB), natural language console commands, Agent/LLM-friendly APIs, real-time WebSocket streaming, and Bloomberg Terminal-inspired multi-column interface. Built with Go and HTMX. Prism extends Terminal with full CAP-67/SEP-41/SEP-50 support. https://github.com/withObsrvr/terminal

Running Infrastructure:

Obsrvr Lake operational on testnet (DigitalOcean) — PostgreSQL hot layer + object storage, processing testnet ledgers in real-time with Bronze/Silver data model.

Mainnet Data Processing (Demonstrated):

Successfully processed live mainnet Soroban contract events using nebu/flowctl cecoding multi-hop DEX swaps, USDC transfers, SAC operations, and token minting in real-time.

Ecosystem Alignment:

Purpose-built for Stellar from day one. Deep familiarity with XDR encoding, Soroban contract architecture, and standards landscape (SEP-41, SEP-50, CAP-67, SEP-39).

Description (from Project):
Latch provides open-source C-address onboarding infrastructure for Stellar, enabling users to create and fund Soroban Smart Accounts without ever touching a traditional G-address. Built on OpenZeppelin's Smart Account standard, Latch delivers three components:

Latch Bridge — A proxy contract and relay service that latches G-addresses to corresponding C-addresses, routing funds from direct sends, CEX withdrawals, and fiat on-ramps to the user's C-address automatically. The G-address layer becomes invisible.

Latch Wallet — A production-grade C-address reference wallet at feature parity with Freighter: full token display, transaction history, signer management, and policy controls.

Latch SDK — TypeScript and Rust libraries for wallet developers to add C-address support instantly, including cross-ecosystem sign-in from MetaMask, Phantom, and Rabby via OpenZeppelin's verifier contracts.
Products & Services:
Latch is an end-to-end, open-source infrastructure stack that eliminates the two adoption blockers preventing C-address (Soroban Smart Account) usage today: the inability to fund a C-address without a G-address, and the absence of production-grade wallet tooling built around the OpenZeppelin Smart Account standard.

The stack is delivered as five decoupled, production-grade components — each a standalone ecosystem primitive, and together a complete onboarding and account management system for any wallet, dApp, or on-ramp that wants to support C-addresses.

1 - Smart Account Contract Suite:  
The on-chain foundation. Three Soroban contracts implementing the full lifecycle of C-address creation, multi-curve authentication, and programmable authorization — built directly on OpenZeppelin's CustomAccountInterface SmartAccount standard.

Smart Account Contract

Implements OpenZeppelin's CustomAccountInterface, SmartAccount, and ExecutionEntryPoint traits. Uses Soroban's native __check_auth hook to enforce programmable, composable authorization organized around three concerns:

-   What (Context Rules): permissions scoped to specific contracts, functions, or any context — with optional expiration by ledger sequence and configurable lifetime.
    
-   Who (Signers): External signers backed by verifier contracts supporting any cryptographic curve — Ed25519, secp256k1, secp256r1/P-256 — plus Delegated signers for G-account and nested C-account composition.
    
-   How (Policies): pluggable enforcement modules for spending limits, multisig thresholds, time restrictions, session-based access, and custom business logic.
    

All authorization logic is enforced on-chain. Trust boundaries are auditable and not dependent on any off-chain service.

Signature Verifier Suite

A family of stateless, immutable verifier contracts implementing OpenZeppelin's Verifier trait — deployed once, shared across all Smart Accounts:

-   Ed25519 Verifier: supports Phantom, any standard Ed25519 hardware key, and any Ed25519-based ecosystem wallet signing authorization payloads directly.
    
-   secp256k1 Verifier: supports MetaMask, Rabby, and any Ethereum-ecosystem wallet. Users sign with wallets they already have on other chains — no new account required.
    
-   WebAuthn / Passkey Verifier: supports Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello, and hardware security keys via the secp256r1/P-256 curve. No seed phrase, no browser extension — just a biometric or device PIN authorizing a Stellar transaction.
    

Swapping the verifier is all it takes to add a new signing scheme. The Smart Account logic stays identical across all of them.

Factory Contract

Deterministically deploys and initializes Smart Accounts in a single call using Soroban's deployer_with_address. Key capabilities:

-   get_c_address(user_pubkey, …) pre-computes the future C-address before deployment, enabling wallets, on-ramps, and escrow rails to fund the account before it exists.
    
-   create_account(user_pubkey, verifier, …) deploys, initializes the Smart Account, and registers the user's chosen signer in one atomic operation.
    
-   Lazy-deploys shared verifier contracts on first use, minimizing per-ecosystem deployment overhead.
    

Outcome: one-call provisioning that turns C-address creation into a single, deterministic primitive — usable by on-ramps, CEX integrations, and wallet providers without any manual contract management.

2 - Latch Bridge (G -> C Forwarding Protocol):

A non-custodial, on-chain forwarding protocol that routes funds from G-addresses directly to C-addresses — making the G-address step transparent to end users.

How it works:

A proxy G-address is generated deterministically from the user's C-address. When funds arrive at that G-address — from a CEX withdrawal, fiat on-ramp, or existing Stellar wallet — a relay service detects the deposit and immediately forwards funds to the target C-address, funding the account for fees in the same operation.

The forwarding logic is verifiable on-chain. The relay is non-custodial — it routes but never holds funds. The user sees only a destination address and a funded account.

Supported funding paths:

-   CEX withdrawal (any exchange supporting Stellar/XLM)
    
-   Fiat on-ramp → G-address → C-address (compatible with existing on-ramp rails)
    
-   Existing Stellar wallet (G-address holders migrating to Smart Accounts)
    
-   Cross-chain bridge integrations for users arriving from other ecosystems
    

Outcome: any existing funding rail becomes a C-address funding rail. The onboarding gap closes entirely — users who have never touched Stellar can land directly in a Smart Account.

3- Latch Wallet (Reference C-address Wallet):  
A production-grade reference wallet implementation demonstrating the full Smart Account tooling stack — at parity with Freighter, built for C-addresses from the ground up.

Core capabilities:

-   Account creation: create a Smart Account using a Passkey (Face ID / Touch ID), an ecosystem wallet (Phantom, MetaMask, Rabby), or a hardware key — zero seed phrase, zero G-address required from the user.
    
-   Token support: full token balances, asset list, and portfolio view for the Smart Account — matching existing Stellar wallet feature parity.
    
-   Transfer history: complete on-chain transaction history scoped to the C-address, including incoming and outgoing transfers across all asset types.
    
-   Send and receive: token transfers authorized via the user's registered signer — Passkey biometric, external wallet signature, or multisig approval flow.
    
-   Session key management: create, view, and revoke scoped session keys aligned with on-chain context rules — giving users visibility into what each dApp can do.
    
-   Gas abstraction: fee sponsorship via Stellar fee bump transactions, so users never need to hold XLM to transact.
    
-   G→C migration flow: guided onboarding for existing Stellar users migrating from a G-address to a Smart Account, with asset bridging built in.
    

Available on web and mobile (React Native).

Outcome: proves end-to-end feasibility, provides a UX baseline for the ecosystem, and serves as the reference implementation against which all SDK integrations are tested.

4 - Latch Onboarding Kit:  
A standard, open-source onboarding kit that any wallet provider or dApp can embed to add C-address support — without reimplementing the flow from scratch.

Pre-built UI components:

-   Signer selection screen (Passkey / MetaMask / Phantom / hardware key)
    
-   G→C migration flow with asset bridging step
    
-   C-address creation confirmation with deterministic address preview

Transaction builders and helpers:

Mobile SDK:

React Native compatible. All core flows — account creation, signing, session management, bridge funding — available on mobile without additional integration work.

Outcome: turns Smart Account onboarding into a copy-paste primitive. A wallet that wants to support C-addresses drops in the SDK, wires up their signer, and ships — without auditing Soroban auth construction from scratch.

5 - Developer Tooling & Documentation:  
The full test suite, reference scripts, and documentation required for the ecosystem to build on Latch confidently.

-   Test suite: unit and integration tests for all three contracts, covering auth flows, verifier correctness, factory deployment, policy enforcement, and edge cases.
    
-   Reference scripts: end-to-end flow scripts in TypeScript demonstrating every integration pattern — CEX funding, multi-signer setup, session key creation, policy configuration — executable against testnet.
    
-   SDK documentation: full API reference, integration guides, and example implementations for web and mobile.
    
-   Audit-ready contracts: contracts structured for external security audit, with documented trust assumptions, access control boundaries, and upgrade policies.
    
-   Ecosystem integration examples: worked integrations showing how an existing Stellar wallet (Freighter-style) and a Soroban dApp each integrate the Latch stack.
Traction Evidence:
We built a working proof of concept demonstrating the core authorization primitive that Latch is built on. The Ed25519 verifier and a Smart Account are deployed on Stellar testnet, with a Next.js demo app showing a Phantom wallet authorizing a Stellar Smart Account transaction end-to-end — no Stellar wallet, no seed phrase, no XLM in the user's hands.

Concretely: the app connects Phantom, reads the user's 32-byte Ed25519 public key, constructs a Stellar authorization payload, passes it to Phantom for signing, packages the raw 64-byte signature into the correct Soroban Signer::External auth entry format, and submits. The on-chain Ed25519 verifier confirms the signature. The Smart Account authorizes the call. Fees are covered by a bundler via Stellar's fee bump mechanism. The full two-phase simulation flow (recording mode → enforcing mode) is implemented correctly — not a shortcut.

3000 Labs is a three-person technical team with consistent, demonstrated depth in the Stellar and Soroban ecosystem. Our track record reflects sustained, hands-on engagement with the ecosystem — not a team arriving fresh to chase a grant.

We have active open source contributions across multiple Stellar ecosystem projects through OnlyDust, GrantFox, and related programs — work that is publicly verifiable on-chain and on GitHub.

We have multiple hackathon wins building on Stellar, most notably at the UnitedEFi hackathon where we built an HTLC smart contract facilitating atomic swaps between an EVM chain and Stellar — a technically demanding cross-chain primitive that required deep understanding of both Soroban contract mechanics and EVM interoperability. Additional wins from the Building on Stellar hackathon track demonstrate consistent execution under competitive conditions.

Our prior Soroban contract work is the direct foundation for this submission. The Smart Account contract suite, verifier architecture, and signing pipeline in Latch are not theoretical — they are extensions of work we have already built, tested, and deployed to testnet.

We are building Latch because we ran into these onboarding problems ourselves. The G-to-C gap is real friction we have experienced directly while building on Stellar. That context informs the design of every component.

Description (from Project):
Our team will create the requested Soroban reverse engineering tool which will take compiled Soroban contracts in WASM format as input and emit accurate WAT, rust and extensive reproducible methods to test its accuracy.  
  
It will follow the listed requirements of:  
  
WASM -> WAT reconstruction that maintains the logic of Soroban contracts while being easily understood for human readers.  
  
Ideally users could simply take a contract ID to initiate the process. The tool will be open source and easily accessible for users, developers and Stellar projects.  
  
Our team is extremely suited for this type of project and we will provide more descriptive details in the following BUILD application.  
  
Please observe our existing research into WASM infrastructures, delivered research and team expertise.
Products & Services:
The Soroban Disassembler is an open-source specialized decompilation tool that can receive the WASM file of a Soroban Smart contract and output easily readable WAT and relevant Rust code. It seeks to create a higher level of accuracy than existing WASM -> WAT conversion tools. 

  
As this proposal is for the Soroban Specialized Reverse Engineering Tool RFP, it will abide by the expected deliverables requested.  
These are detailed further in the Technical Architecture document

Submission / Project: RFP: Soroban (WASM) Specialized Reverse Engineering Tool
Description (from Project):
In SCF RFPs section: https://stellar.gitbook.io/scf-handbook/scf-awards/build-award/rfp-track#soroban-specialized-reverse-engineering-tool  
A special WASM->WAT tool has been described, which I personally found immense need for while working on Solang (Solidity to WASM compiler). In summary, the tool should be able to:  
- Convert Soroban specific WASM files to WAT, in which the WAT is human readable via embedding knowledge about the soroban host functions or imported utilities in the tool itself.  
- The produced WAT should be more human-readbale and accurate than the standard WASM2WAT tool  
- reconstructed WAT should be as close to the original source as possible  
  
  
Note: before such tool exists, what I would personally do to trace a WAT file would be a mix of syntax highlighted WAT, mapping the host function names(i.e i._) to human readable names and finally using some AI chatbot (which is not really accurate for debugging or auditing purposes)
Project: RFP: Soroban (WASM) Specialized Reverse Engineering Tool
Products & Services: We offer a specialized reverse engineering tool that accepts a compiled Soroban Smart Contract (WASM file) and produces a highly accurate and human-readable WebAssembly Text (WAT) representation, alongside compilable Rust code.
Traction Evidence:
This submission is in response to this RFP: Soroban Specialized Reverse Engineering Tool.  
What took my attention in the first place was that I work on Solang: A Solidity compiler to Soroban. Throughout my work, I have found a real need for a tool that analyses emitted Soroban WASM contracts.  
What I, as an SDK dev, would do to analyze an emitted WASM contract is look at the corresponding WAT and face those challenegs:  
1. Manually mapping host calls from https://github.com/stellar/rs-soroban-env/blob/main/soroban-env-common/env.json  
2. If the contract does undesired behavior, I would have to trace generic WAT :)  
3. If I wanted to see "how would the SDK do X functionality" I would either check the rs-soroban-sdk code base or analyze its emitted WAT as well  
  
Traction of course cannot be proved by describing my pain points. However, since this is an RFP submission, I though this is the place to describe my pain points anyways :)
Tranche 1 - Deliverables:
Brief Description:  
Parse a WASM Soroban contract, insert Soroban related facts then emit CFG/SSA IR (via waffle).

Deliverables:  
1. Parse WASM and produce WasmFacts and ParseDiagnostics.

2. Decode contractspecv0, contractmetav0, and contractenvmetav0 into SorobanFacts and MetadataDiagnostics.

3. Lift WASM to CFG/SSA IR via waffle and produce LiftedIR plus LiftDiagnostics.

4. Add baseline semantic recovery for core host calls and publish initial test vectors and scoring rules.

How to measure completion:  
1. The tool should be able to print human readable waffle CFG/SSA IR  
2. The tool should emit WasmFacts and ParseDiagnostics
Tranche 2 - Testnet:
Brief Description:  
Take in waffle’s CFG+SSA IR + Soroban recovered semantics, and restructure it into a typed, AST like program with explicit control flow and ANF-style bindings.  
  
Deliverables and how to measure completion:  
The tool should be able to emit a human readable, annotated WAT with Soroban specifics.
Tranche 3 - Mainnet:
Brief Description:

Emit compilable Rust reconstruction from HighIR, and validate quality with AST-based comparison against source.  
  

Deliverables:

1.Rust emitter that generates compilable analysis-grade Rust.

2.AST comparison pipeline (macro-expanded + canonicalized) for source vs reconstructed Rust.

3.Final benchmark suite and report targeting >=90% reconstruction score on agreed vectors.

4.Final docs: limits, confidence interpretation, compatibility matrix.  

How to Measure Completion:

Generated Rust passes cargo check on benchmark vectors.

AST comparison tool outputs per-contract and aggregate scores.

Final published report shows >=90% on agreed vectors and category breakdown (interface/structure/semantic/behavior).

Requested Edits 7.0:
'- The ingestion depends on Horizon and RPC endpoints for Stellar ledger and Soroban events, introducing single-point failure risks and contradicting RFP intent for robust, decentralized processing e.g. via Galexie or other Composable Data Platform tooling. 
- It is not clear which service provider the team will rely on, or if self-hosting these dependencies is within the submission scope.
- Replace Horizon and RPC dependencies with direct ledger processing (e.g., via Galexie or equivalent tooling) for ingestion of Stellar trades and Soroban events to ensure decentralized reliability OR clearly state how you will mitigate against Horizon and RPC failures. We're looking for a Prices API that keeps working even when other infrastructure fails.
- Provide a detailed itemized budget breakdown (e.g. hours per phase, division of labor and infra costs) to justify the $138k request against RFP scope and SCF value standards.
- Detail concrete implementation plans for all required oracles (Chainlink, Redstone, Band, Reflector) and liquidity venues (Soroswap, Aquarius, SDEX, Blend), including handling of protocol-specific data. Any data/ingestion sources that are not clearly detailed must be presented as aspirational (stretch goals) and not guaranteed to be part of the deliverables.
Description (from Project):
We wish to deliver against the Prices API RFP - we already operate a pricing API that has served hundreds of millions of free queries with 99.99% uptime. It powers multiple wallets and our own payments system. That said, it reflects the era it was originally built in around 2020. While we’ve modernized parts of it, it would need a substantial upgrade to meet enterprise grade expectations today.  

The current system aggregates data from 16 sources, removes outliers, supports source weighting, and can generate composite rates. For example, to calculate XLM:BRL it may derive a route via XLM:BTC and BTC:BRL, including multi step paths where necessary. We also support a nested query mode that exposes underlying source data so downstream developers can apply their own logic, alongside a more opinionated single rate endpoint.  
  
You can see an example query here: https://rates.ctx.com/rates?symbol=xlmeur&nested=1
Products & Services:
> Update Note - 27 February 2026: We've made some updates and clarifications in response to reviewer feedback. Changes address: (1) Horizon and RPC dependency risks - we will self-host all Stellar node infrastructure across our colocated and cloud environments with full redundancy, and will evaluate Galexie and the Composable Data Platform, our general approach is to always go low level and we'll either read from ledgers, use existing tooling (CDP) or even implement the protocol itself; (2) an itemized budget breakdown; and (3) concrete implementation plans for all required oracles and liquidity venues. We have no plans to rely on third-party hosted node providers - our existing infrastructure stack and colocated hardware give us the foundation to run everything ourselves. All eight required sources (Chainlink, Redstone, Band Protocol, Reflector, Soroswap, Aquarius, SDEX, Blend) are planned deliverables; integration approaches for each are now detailed in the technical architecture document.  

Additionally I would like to make the following clarifications:  
- We're committed to delivering the full RFP & Freighter RFQ to above and beyond there specification. There will be some discovery along this build process and we'll definitely evaluate the full composable data platform and Galaxie and document our findings and will use them where suitable (this may involve forks and/or pull requests too).  
  
- We have no intention of relying on third party providers, this involves more risk and one of the core benefits of our experience is in our infrastructure knowledge and existing stack; we will be deploying multiple nodes ourselves (colocation and cloud) with full failover, everything will be designed from the ground-up to keep working as a reliable long term pricing partner with graceful degradation and rapid restoration in the event of any issues (network issues like the P23 deployment stability issues, AWS/cloudflare outages, Oracle freeze and so on). If we do integrate third party data providers (Data lake/nodes/otherwise) that will be more as a redundancy to our main self-hosted approach. 

- Before recent events I highlighted my concerns around supply chain attack risk to a couple of team members, this is a critical piece of infrastructure that needs to be built right, whilst we will have it in production per the timeline a third party audit of our methodology around pricing would be very logical - especially if we then additionally publish data on chain as a secondary oracle to Reflector down the line. As we've built for cross-chain decentralized exchanges before we know these attack vectors well so I am confident we'll approach this right but having additional eyes on the project would be very advisable and we would gladly make use of the audit bank support. CTX.com has provided real-time pricing data since 2020, serving millions of low-latency queries across hundreds of asset pairs with greater than 99.99 percent uptime. Our infrastructure currently powers wallets with over a million downloads, payment service providers, and DeFi applications. The service has operated reliably and free of charge for more than five years.

This project upgrades that proven system into a comprehensive, enterprise-ready pricing infrastructure purpose-built for the Stellar ecosystem. The new platform will deliver real-time and historical pricing across all Stellar assets, including both Classic and Soroban tokens. Assets will be pairable against other Stellar assets, BTC, and more than 100 fiat currencies, with USD serving as the normalization anchor.

In addition to the 16 real-time data sources currently integrated, this expansion will include full indexing of Stellar DEX trades, Soroban AMM and DEX activity such as Soroswap, Aquarius, SDEX, and Blend, and integration of all relevant oracle networks. The system is designed with modular ingestion pipelines, enabling the addition of new centralized exchanges, decentralized markets, and oracle providers without changes to core aggregation logic.

All components will be fully open source. Pricing data will be available via a public API. Free rate-limited API access will be provided, with unlimited API keys available at no cost to SDF and SCF-supported teams. Comprehensive documentation, container images, and deployment scripts will be published to support self-hosted deployments.

Please refer to the accompanying technical architecture document for detailed specifications and implementation design.
Traction Evidence:
CTX Rates has been operating in production for over five years, delivering real-time pricing across hundreds of asset pairs and serving millions of low-latency queries. The system currently ingests data from 16 independent sources, applies outlier filtering, supports source weighting, and generates composite cross-asset rates through deterministic aggregation logic.

The infrastructure has powered pricing for tens of millions of transactions and has been relied upon consistently by wallets, payment processors, and DeFi applications. It has maintained greater than 99.99 percent uptime throughout this period.

This project builds on a mature, battle-tested system with demonstrated reliability, operational stability, and real-world usage across multiple production integrations.

Description (from Project):
As the first institutional vault infrastructure provider on Stellar, we have been working on portfolio position tracking and net asset value calculation. Our portfolio NAV module enables vault curators to have real-time, accurate PnL and share price calculation for their vaults. We are working with Thaw Capital (Indentura), Stake Capital, Gami Capital and soon Sentora on their first-ever vaults on Stellar.  
  
With this project, we are making this institutional-grade infrastructure available to anyone on Stellar. Anyone can query their portfolio value down to individual position level with just a G or C address.

We solved multiple issues in building this tool. For example, Blend values dToken (debt) at a different USD price from that of Aquarius. Whilst this is ok for Blend as they only need to care about XLM amount when users repay, it creates mismatch portfolio PnL in USD terms. So consistent pricing source/oracle is important in position valuation.  

From this foundation, we can extend/build OctoPos in much less than 10 weeks.  
  
OctoPos - think of it as "DeBank" for Stellar.
Products & Services:
Today, users on Stellar lack a single source of truth for their portfolio value. Wallets do not consistently display positions — debt positions are often invisible, and even supply positions may not be fully reflected. And this is when interacting with just a single protocol. Once users attempt to implement more sophisticated strategies, such as delta-neutral structures, the challenge extends beyond position tracking to accurate, real-time valuation. This fragmentation presents a major obstacle to deploying yield strategies, building personal finance applications, and enabling AI agents to rebalance portfolios effectively, to mention a few.

OctoPos is a production-ready DeFi Position Aggregation API for the Stellar ecosystem — 'DeBank for Stellar'. It normalises, calculates and displays a user's full DeFi exposure across all major Stellar protocols from a single G-address or C-address query.

Current tracking capability covers all required RFP data points — Total current value, Total deposit value, Total borrowed value, Total current return, Health factor and moer — as visible in live production apps above. The RFP award funds extension of this infrastructure to any Stellar address (not just vault curators) and additional protocol depth, in the following main areas:

-   Track positions for any address not just those by our curators
    
-   Integrate with more protocols (note we already integrated with the major ones)
    
-   Improve performance making it highly available, low-latency, and capable of handling high query volume. 
    
-   Build historical performance of all addresses
    
-   Expose API and docs so anyone can use
    

The project will be shipped with the following 5 features:

Feature 1: Multi-Protocol Position Aggregation

Query any Stellar wallet address to retrieve a unified view of all DeFi positions across Blend, Aquarius, SoroSwap, Phoenix Hub, Untangled Vault and more. Each position is normalised to USD using a consistent, multi-source oracle strategy (Aquarius AMM swap quotes + Blend Protocol oracle + Stellar Expert), eliminating cross-protocol pricing mismatches (e.g., dToken valuation differences between Blend and Aquarius).

Stellar Usage: Queries Stellar Horizon for account data, Soroban RPC for contract state, Blend SDK for lending positions, and Aquarius AMM for LP positions. All data is fetched on-chain, directly from Stellar mainnet.

Impact: Enables vault curators and retail users alike to have accurate, real-time PnL and portfolio views without building custom integrations per protocol.

Feature 2: Required Data Points — Full Coverage

For each position, OctoPos exposes: Total current value, Total deposit value, Total borrowed value (where applicable), Total current return / yield, Health factor / liquidation risk metrics, and Position value history. This is live today for Loop positions and Vault NAV — the RFP award will extend it to all Stellar protocols and any address.

Stellar Usage: Health factor computed using Blend SDK reserve factors (Collateral Factor, Liability Factor) fetched from Soroban contracts. Historical snapshots stored in MongoDB with Kubernetes CronJobs running on a 60-second tracker cycle.

Feature 3: REST API with Self-Service Onboarding

A publicly documented REST API (modelled on DeBank and Nansen standards) with endpoints for account summary, per-position breakdown, position history, event log, and protocol-level stats. No authentication required for public data.

Stellar Usage: Base URL served from ; all data sourced from Stellar mainnet via Horizon and Soroban RPC. Existing Loop endpoints already follow this pattern (e.g., GET /loop/account/:account/summary).

Impact: Any developer can build portfolio dashboards, analytics tools, or personal finance apps on top of OctoPos without writing a single line of Stellar integration code.

Feature 4: Historical Position Tracking

Position snapshots stored at 60-second intervals (active positions) and every 2 hours for vault NAV. MongoDB time-series collections (looppositions, looppositionevents, looppositionsnapshots) enable charting of equity, PnL, health factor, and yield over any time range — comparable to Nansen's wallet profiler.

Stellar Usage: Event history sourced from Stellar blockchain events via Horizon API. Position lifecycle (Opened → Open → Reduced → Closed / Liquidated) fully tracked on-chain.

Feature 5: Resilient, High-Availability Infrastructure

Multi-source price feeds with automatic fallback (Aquarius AMM → Blend Oracle → Stellar Expert → last cached price). Circuit-breaker pattern: if one protocol source fails, the API continues serving data from the remaining sources and logs degraded state. Kubernetes-based deployment with 60-second background position tracker, 60-second price cache TTL, and Slack alerting on failures.

Stellar Usage: Deduplication logic prevents double-counting when the same asset appears across multiple Stellar protocols (e.g., XLM appearing in both Stellar account balance and Blend collateral). The highest-value source is retained per asset, per the existing NAV module philosophy.

Description (from Project): A Kotlin Multiplatform SDK for building applications on the Stellar Network. Write your Stellar integration once in Kotlin and deploy it across JVM (Android, Server), iOS, macOS, and Web (Browser/Node.js) platforms.
Products & Services: Smart account support for the Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) Stellar SDK. The project adds passkey-based wallet creation, signer management, context rules, policy enforcement, and token transfers for OpenZeppelin smart account contracts on Stellar. It targets Android, iOS, macOS, and web using native platform APIs for WebAuthn and secure storage. The SDK also includes a multiplatform demo app and documentation covering usage, platform setup, onboarding concepts, and a full API reference.

The KMP Stellar SDK covers transaction building, Horizon, Soroban RPC, a high-level ContractClient, and support for multiple SEPs. It runs on JVM (Android, server), iOS, macOS, and JavaScript (browser, Node.js). The SDKs are used by third-party developers and applications in the Stellar ecosystem.

Description (from Project):
StellarPasskey Cloud is an API-as-a-Service platform that enables applications on the Stellar network to integrate Passkey authentication (WebAuthn) and provision C-Addresses (Soroban Smart Accounts) through a simple SDK and standardized onboarding kit.

As Stellar transitions toward C-Addresses and smart accounts via Soroban, builders face friction around authentication, account provisioning, funding flows, fee handling, and secure transaction signing. Our goal is to reduce integration time to hours instead of weeks.

The MVP will deliver:

-   Passkey registration and verification (WebAuthn)
    
-   Automatic C-Address provisioning per user
    
-   Transaction signing via passkeys
    
-   A transparent G-step → C-Address funding flow
    
-   A JS/TS SDK
    
-   An open-source Onboarding Kit and reference wallet app
    

We aim to standardize modern onboarding patterns so any Stellar app can offer “Login with Passkey + Smart Account” without rebuilding core infrastructure.
Products & Services:
Passkey Module: WebAuthn credential registration, payload signing, and secure rotation and recovery flows.

C-Address Module: Deployment and management of smart accounts (Soroban), including credential registration and enforcement of policies (spending limits, allowlists, and recovery mechanisms).

Funding Module (G→C): Abstraction layer for initial funding flows, supporting CEX withdrawals, bridges, and mechanisms designed to minimize user exposure to the G-step.

Off-ramp Module (C→G): Standardized exit flows to ensure interoperability with anchors, CEXs, and legacy infrastructure.

Transaction & Account Helpers: Client-side transaction construction, simulation, and submission.

Reference Wallet: Functional interface (tokens, transaction history, and account state) for validating flows in a near-production environment.

Onboarding Toolkit: Definition of a standardized end-to-end flow covering account creation, funding, asset management, and XLM provisioning for transaction fees.

-  

The outcomes will inform technical and product decisions, including:

-   Definition of the POC scope and functional prioritization
    
-   Prioritization and composition of SDK modules (passkeys, account abstraction, funding, and off-ramp)
    
-   Specification of Soroban contracts, including security policies, recovery mechanisms, and credential validation
    
-   Definition of observability, security, and client-side risk control requirements
Traction Evidence:
The project is currently at a pre-MVP stage, with no quantitative adoption metrics yet (e.g., users or TVL). However, it demonstrates strong qualitative traction and a high level of execution readiness, supported by three main pillars:

-  

Execution capacity and ecosystem alignment:  
The team brings over five years of experience building blockchain infrastructure and educational programs across the ecosystem. Its track record includes developing projects and initiatives for major institutions such as the Central Bank of Brazil, Visa, and XP Bank, among others.

Beyond institutional work, the team has maintained active leadership within the Stellar ecosystem—leading initiatives, participating in hackathons, organizing large-scale events, and building projects aligned with the Soroban roadmap. This combination of enterprise-grade delivery and ecosystem-native execution reinforces both technical depth and operational maturity.

-  

Problem validation:  
Qualitative evidence gathered from builders indicates that C-address onboarding—particularly around authentication (passkeys), initial funding (XLM), and first transaction execution—remains non-standardized and carries high integration costs. These recurring friction points validate the need for a modular, SDK-first approach.

-  

Incremental validation strategy:  
During MVP development, a closed beta will be conducted with selected ecosystem projects to validate the SDK-first architecture, iterate on core modules (passkeys, account abstraction, funding/off-ramp), and consolidate the Onboarding Toolkit with end-to-end examples and technical documentation.

Additionally, the project includes the release of public artifacts (repositories, documentation, and a reference application) to enable open validation and accelerate adoption across the ecosystem.

Submission / Project: Stellar DEX Liquidity Engine - Hummingbot Integration
Description (from Project): We're building a Stellar DEX connector for Hummingbot, the most widely used open source algorithmic trading framework. Kelp's deprecation left Stellar without a maintained market making tool, resulting in thin books, wide spreads, prices that drift from centralized venues during any vol event. Instead of building another standalone tool that depends on one maintainer and eventually dies, we're plugging Stellar into a platform with 15,000+ active instances, 50+ exchange connectors, and built in strategies that real desks use: market making, cross exchange arb, grid, TWAP, VWAP. The core connector handles full order management, real time state tracking, local orderbook maintenance, parallel transaction submission via channel accounts, and support for any trading pair, all built against Stellar RPC. The finished connector gets merged into the official Hummingbot repo, not shipped as a fork. Optional scope includes Soroban AMM support for Aquarius, Soroswap, and intra AMM arbitrage, and this ultimately results in more market makers, tighter spreads, prices that reflect reality.
Project: Stellar DEX Liquidity Engine - Hummingbot Integration

Traction Evidence:
1. Ecosystem Mandate (RFP)

This project is a direct response to the Open RFP for Hummingbot Integration, addressing a critical infrastructure gap identified by the Stellar ecosystem. The deprecation of Kelp has left the network without a maintained, institutional-grade market-making tool, resulting in thinner order books and higher spreads. Consequently, this proposal is not a search for product-market fit, but a targeted effort to restore essential trading infrastructure and liquidity provision capabilities to the network as explicitly requested by the foundation.

2. Technical Feasibility & Architectural Validation

We have proactively validated our architectural approach by auditing the Stellar SDK against Hummingbot's core Exchange class requirements, confirming that Stellar RPC (specifically utilizing getEvents for event streaming) provides the necessary low-latency fidelity to maintain the real-time, local order book required by the bot. To mitigate development risk and ensure institutional-grade performance, we are utilizing the existing XRPL connector as a proven blueprint for non-EVM ledger integration and have designed a custom Channel Account Pool system to resolve sequence number concurrency, solving the specific throughput bottlenecks that previously limited high-frequency trading on the network.

Additionally, our team possesses a track record of building complex, mathematically rigorous trading primitives on other chains. On Monad, we blueprinted a production-grade VWAP Execution Engine that leverages parallel execution patterns to minimize institutional slippage, logic that directly conforms with the high-throughput requirements of this Stellar connector. On Base, we've created a sophisticated prediction market liquidity engine that utilizes transformer-based semantic analysis and LightGBM price-pattern modeling to compute complex event correlations via Gaussian copula mathematics, executing the resulting risk-priced positions trustlessly on-chain through EIP-712 signatures. These builds demonstrate our ability to deliver the stable, scalable, and sophisticated infrastructure required to restore professional market-making capabilities to the Stellar ecosystem.

3. Team Validation, Markets Backgrounds, and Engineering Profiles

Our team architects and maintains production-grade automation infrastructure at Coinbase, where we manage architecture for high-throughput financial systems that demand absolute data consistency and zero-defect execution. We possess end-to-end expertise across the entire product lifecycle, from navigating complex stakeholder requirements to shipping battle-tested applications to production. Beyond enterprise engineering, we bring deep, practical mastery of both DeFi and TradFi market microstructure. We have successfully architected and executed profitable on-chain arbitrage and market-making strategies on-chain, giving us an intuitive grasp of the precise latency, sequence management, and reliability requirements a Hummingbot connector needs to succeed.

Additionally, this proposal is the direct result of conversations we've had with members of the Stellar team and community at ETHDenver. After discussing our backgrounds and recognizing the critical need to create standardized liquidity solutions, members of the community highly recommended that we look into and apply to this RFP. We are more than happy to go into specific projects we've shipped, our extensive experience within TradFi/DeFi, and how we plan to leverage our backgrounds to restore institutional-grade market making capabilities to the Stellar network, ultimately delivering a solution that far exceeds the capabilities of previous tooling like Kelp.
Tranche 1 - Deliverables:
Tranche 1: Core Gateway Architecture & Read-Only Connectivity

By the end of this tranche, the Hummingbot Gateway will be able to run in a Docker container, connect to Stellar Testnet/Mainnet, and stream live order book data with sub-2-second latency.

#### Deliverable 1.1: Gateway Scaffolding & Containerization

The first deliverable focuses on initializing the Hummingbot Gateway repository (TypeScript/Node.js) and configure the essential server infrastructure required to communicate with the core Hummingbot Client. 

Goals:

-   Setup the Express.js server with standard Hummingbot middleware (cert-based authentication, logging, configuration routing)
    
-   Create the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml configurations to ensure the connector runs in an isolated environment (critical for potential institutional deployment)
    
-   Implement the logic to handle stellar.yml configurations (Network Passphrase, RPC endpoints, Horizon URLs) securely
    

Technical Justification: Hummingbot requires a specific HTTP server standard to recognize a new connector. Without this handshake layer (certs, auth, standard routes, etc), the Python client cannot communicate with the Stellar integration/

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   Public GitHub repository initialized with the Gateway structure
    
-   Calling GET /status on the Gateway returns 200 OK and correctly reports the Stellar network status
    
-   A video showing the Gateway booting up via docker-compose up without errors
    

Budget: $5,500

#### Deliverable 1.2: Hybrid Network Layer (RPC + Horizon)  

The second deliverable focuses on implementing the dual-client connection logic. We will not rely solely on Horizon (which is too slow for market making), instead, we will build a hybrid class that uses Soroban RPC for low-latency event streaming and Horizon for static data.  

Goals:

-   Implement the WebSocket/HTTP2 connection to Soroban RPC to listen for getEvents
    
-   Build the lookup logic that maps Stellar Asset objects (Native, AlphaNum4, AlphaNum12) into Hummingbot’s standardized TradingPair format (e.g., XLM-USDC)
    
-   Create the get_tickers logic that polls the backend to fetch 24h volume and price data for the discovery dashboard
    

Technical Justification: Standard Horizon polling (2-5 seconds) guarantees a market maker will quote stale prices and lose money. This hybrid layer is necessary to ingest data at the speed of the ledger (sub-2s) while keeping the reliability of Horizon for asset metadata.  

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   A log comparing a Horizon poll timestamp vs. an RPC event timestamp, demonstrating the speed advantage
    
-   The /network/stellar/status endpoint returns valid connection metrics for both RPC and Horizon
    

Budget: $8,000

#### Deliverable 1.3: XDR Decoding & Order Book Snapshot Engine

In the third deliverable, we will build the translator that converts raw Stellar transaction data into a standard order book.

Goals: 

-   Implement a native TypeScript decoder using @stellar/stellar-sdk to parse ManageBuyOffer and ManageSellOffer operations from the raw stream
    
-   Build an in-memory data structure that maintains the state of the order book (Bids/Asks) and updates it incrementally based on the XDR stream
    
-   Implement logic to snapshot the book every 5 seconds (on finality) to ensure the bot doesn't react to incomplete data  
    

Technical Justification: Hummingbot expects a standard price and amount format. Stellar sends raw XDR, and if this translation layer is inaccurate, the bot will misprice assets. Since LCP handling is unique to Stellar, we must ensure the bot waits for finality before recalculating its strategy.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   Running the Hummingbot order_book command in the terminal and seeing real-time Bids/Asks for XLM-USDC on Testnet
    
-   Automated test script that compares the gateway's local order book against a Stellar expert snapshot to prove data integrity
    

Budget: $11,000

Tranche 1 Summary

-   3 deliverables (Scaffolding, Network Layer, XDR Engine)
    
-   Creation of a strong initial foundation and conforming Hummingbot for Stellar
    
-   Total budget of $24,500
Tranche 2 - Testnet:
Tranche 2: High-Performance Execution & Concurrency Engine

By the end of this tranche, the system will be able to submit, cancel, and modify orders at high frequency (5+ TPS) without triggering tx_bad_seq errors, enabling true market-making strategies.

#### Deliverable 2.1: Core Transaction Builder & Signing Service  

The first deliverable will implement the write logic for the Gateway. This module translates Hummingbot's generic trade instructions into valid, signed Stellar XDR transactions.

Goals: 

-   Implement the logic to construct ManageBuyOffer, ManageSellOffer, and PathPaymentStrictReceive operations
    
-   Integrate with Soroban RPC to fetch real-time inclusion fees (dynamic fee bidding) to ensure transactions land in the next pool during congestion
    
-   Implement the UserStreamTracker to monitor XLM/Token balances and critically calculate spendable balance (total balance minus base reserve minus trustline reserves) so the bot doesn't accidentally brick the account
    

Technical Justification: Stellar’s reserve mechanism is unique. If a bot tries to trade its entire balance, the transaction fails, and this module prevents any insufficient balance loops by mathematically enforcing reserve limits before a trade is submitted.  

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   A test suite proving the reserve_calculator correctly identifies the spendable amount for an account with 3 trustlines and 2 open offers
    
-   Transaction hashes showing the bot successfully creating a generic limit order on the DEX
    
-   Logs showing the bot adjusting its fee bid (e.g., from 100 stroops to 150 stroops) in response to network surge
    

Budget: $18,200

#### Deliverable 2.2: Channel Account Manager (Concurrency Engine)

The second deliverable will enable us to build a pool management system that allows the bot to bypass the one transaction per account limit.  

Goals: 

-   Create a ChannelManager class that initializes and manages a pool of 5-10 subsidiary channel accounts (funded with min-reserve XLM)
    
-   Implement the rotation logic, where when the bot wants to submit a trade, it grabs an idle channel account to use as the source acount (for sequence number consumption) while the operation source remains the main trading wallet
    
-   Build a self-healing mechanism, and if a transaction fails due to tx_bad_seq (sequence collision), the manager immediately re-signs it with a fresh channel account and retries within millisecond
    

Technical Justification: Without this, a market maker is dead on arrival. If the bot tries to cancel 5 orders and place 5 new ones in a single block, 9 of those 10 transactions would fail on a standard setup. This engine enables parallel execution, allowing the bot to refresh its entire spread continiously.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   A screen recording of the bot submitting 5 simultaneous orders (Buy/Sell) in a single attempt. We will verify this by showing the block explorer link where all 5 txs appear in the same ID
    
-   A log file demonstrating the system intentionally triggering a sequence error and automatically recovering/retrying without user intervention
    
-   Prometheus metrics showing a sustained rate of >1 transaction per second over a 10-minute window
    

Budget: $24,320

Tranche 2 Summary

-   2 deliverables (Transaction Builder, Channel Manager)
    
-   Creation and implementation of the parallel execution feature via channel accounts
    
-   Total budget of $42,520
Tranche 3 - Mainnet:
Tranche 3: Intelligence, Soroban & Mainnet Launch

By the end of this tranche, we will move from theoretical functionality to actual profitability. The bot will be able to access Soroban liquidity, backtest strategies against realistic Stellar ledger mechanics, and run safely on Mainnet.

#### Deliverable 3.1: Soroban AMM Liquidity Aggregator

The first deliverable is focused on liquidity on Stellar, as it is currently fragmented between the Classic Order Book (CLOB) and Soroban AMMs (Aquarius, Soroswap, Phoenix). We will build the aggregation layer to unify these streams and replicate the TradFi equivalent of NBBO.

Goals:

-   Implement the RPC logic to fetch reserve balances from Soroban AMM contracts (utilizing get_ledger_entries for contract storage)
    
-   Convert AMM x * y = k curves into a virtual order Book format that Hummingbot can understand. This allows the bot to see AMM liquidity as if it were a standard Bid/Ask spread
    
-   Implement the logic to compare prices of Pclob vs Pamm. If the AMM price is better (accounting for gas/fees), the bot routes the trade to the smart contract instead of the DEX
    

Technical Justification: Without this, the bot is blind to a high amount of the network's liquidity. A professional market maker must be able to arbitrage between the fixed-price AMMs and the floating-price order book to keep markets efficient.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   A video showing the bot detecting a price discrepancy and executing a buy on AMM / Sell on DEX atomic arbitrage transaction on Testnet
    
-   The Gateway API /price endpoint returns a best execution quote that checks both venues
    

Budget: $18,400

#### Deliverable 3.2: Predictive LCP (Ledger Close Protocol) Backtesting Engine

The second deliverable is centered around the a high-fidelity simulation environment designed to accurately model strategy performance under Stellar's unique consensus constraints. Standard Hummingbot backtesting assumes continuous time (trades happen instantly), but Stellar works in 5-Second Batches. We will build a custom simulation engine to model this.

Goals:

-   A Python module that ingests historical Stellar ledger data and replays it in 5-second discrete windows
    
-   A statistical model that estimates the probability of a trade being included in Ledger N vs Ledger N+1 based on submission timestamp and fee density
    
-   We will run heavy simulations in different environments to determine the optimal inventory skew parameters for Stellar's specific volatility profile
    

Technical Justification: If you backtest a High-Frequency Trading strategy on continuous time but deploy it on a 5-second discrete ledger, you will lose money. This engine prevents overfitting by simulating the actual constraints of the Stellar network and backtests it very aggressively to ensure profitability.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   Release of the stellar_lcp_backtest Python library.
    
-   Elaborate documentation of standard vs. LCP Backtesting and how latency modeling saved x% in slippage
    
-   A script allowing reviewers to run the backtest themselves on historical data
    

Budget: $22,400

#### Deliverable 3.3: Mainnet Hardening & Educational Suite

The final deliverable involves security parameter tuning, documentation, and the creation of the Stellar Humingbot series to ensure user adoption.

Goals:

-   Pre-tuned conf_global.yml templates for Mainnet, including safe mode defaults (kill-switch thresholds, max-drawdown limits)
    
-   Final code cleanup, removal of debug logs, and extensive commenting to prepare for the security audit (audit cost excluded)
    
-   Extensive documentation via gitbooks or notion to enable a user to walk through the entire process by themselves
    
-   Production of multiple high-quality video tutorials (some examples here)
    
    a.  "Zero to Hero: Setting up and Installing Stellar Hummingbot on Mac/Windows"
        
    a.  "Running Your First Market Making Bot on XLM/USDC for beginners"
        
    a.  A series of advanced videos "ex. Arbitrage & Channel Accounts Explained"
        

Technical Justification: Great code without easy to follow documentation results in high barriers of entry for users. To achieve the mandate of restoring easy to access market making platforms and allowing anyone to provide liquidity, we must make this tool accessible to non-engineers.

Validation / Acceptance Criteria:

-   A video of the bot placing real-money trades on Stellar Mainnet, with transactions showing up in the mainnet block explorer
    
-   A live docs.hummingbot.org style site specifically for the Stellar Connector
    
-   The official pull request to the Hummingbot Foundation repository
    

Budget: $13,800

Tranche 3 Summary

-   3 deliverables (Soroban Aggregator, Backtesting Engine, Mainnet Launch)
    
-   Putting all the pieces together on mainnet and ensuring the protocol is ready for audit and launch
    
-   Total Budget of $54,600

Description (from Project):
SmoothSend is a multi-chain gasless transaction infrastructure that lets apps sponsor fees so users can send tokens without holding native gas. We support Aptos, EVM chains, and Stellar.

On Stellar, we use Fee Bump Transactions (CAP-0015) so users can send XLM and USDC without XLM for fees. We also support claimable balances and BeginSponsoringFutureReserves, so users can send USDC to new wallets that have never held XLM. The relayer sponsors account creation and trustlines.

Developers integrate via a 3-line SDK (@smoothsend/sdk on npm), with a single API key and dashboard for usage, billing, and API keys. SmoothSend removes the “need XLM for gas” barrier for payments and onboarding on Stellar.
Products & Services:
SmoothSend – Products & Services
====================================

1. SmartAccount Contract (Soroban)
---------------------------------------

What: A Soroban smart contract that represents a user's C-address. Each user gets their own deployed instance. The contract holds tokens (XLM, USDC, EURC, any SAC asset) and only the owner's G-address can authorize transfers.

Stellar Usage:

-   Deployed via deploy_v2 on Soroban
    
-   Uses SAC (Stellar Asset Contract) for token operations
    
-   owner.require_auth() for all state-changing operations
    
-   Compatible with OpenZeppelin Smart Account standard
    

Impact: Enables C-addresses to be first-class wallets on Stellar, holding and sending any Stellar asset without a G-address.

-  

2. CAddressFactory Contract (Soroban)
------------------------------------------

What: Singleton factory contract that deploys SmartAccount instances deterministically. Same owner always gets the same C-address (CREATE2-style via sha256(owner)). Idempotent — calling twice returns the existing C-address.

Stellar Usage:

-   env.deployer().with_current_contract(salt).deploy_v2(wasm_hash, args) for deterministic deployment
    
-   env.crypto().sha256() for salt computation
    
-   Persistent storage for owner→C-address mapping
    
-   Admin-controlled WASM hash upgrades
    

Impact: Provides a standard, predictable way for any wallet or dApp to create C-addresses. Pre-computable addresses enable receiving funds before deployment.

-  

3. G-to-C Bridge Relayer
-----------------------------

What: TypeScript service that bridges the gap between traditional G-address infrastructure and C-addresses. Builds Soroban transactions for G→C and C→G/C transfers, handles fee-bump payment so users never pay gas.

Key Endpoints:

-   POST /bridge-transfer — Build G→C transfer via SAC
    
-   POST /build-transfer — Build C→any transfer via SmartAccount.send()
    
-   POST /submit-transfer — Fee-bump wrap + submit
    
-   GET /balance/:cAddress — Multi-token balances (XLM, USDC, EURC)
    
-   GET /history/:cAddress — Transaction history via Soroban events
    
-   POST /create — Deploy new SmartAccount via Factory
    
-   GET /lookup/:gAddress — Find C-address by owner
    

Stellar Usage:

-   @stellar/stellar-sdk v14.5.0 with rpc.Server for Soroban RPC
    
-   assembleTransaction for resource estimation
    
-   TransactionBuilder.buildFeeBumpTransaction() for gasless UX
    
-   Soroban getEvents for transfer history
    
-   SAC contract invocations for all token operations
    

Impact: Makes C-addresses accessible to any developer — no Soroban knowledge required. Handles all the complexity of Soroban transaction building, simulation, and fee payment.

-  

4. SDK — @smoothsend/sdk v2.0.0
------------------------------------------

What: TypeScript SDK with StellarCAddressAdapter — a dedicated module for all C-address operations. Published on npm as @smoothsend/sdk.

Key Features:

-   create(ownerGAddress) — Deploy C-address
    
-   lookup(ownerGAddress) — Find existing C-address
    
-   getBalances(cAddress) — Multi-token balances
    
-   buildBridgeTransfer(params) — Build G→C transfer
    
-   buildTransfer(params) — Build C→any transfer
    
-   submitTransfer(signedXDR) — Fee-bump submit
    
-   getHistory(cAddress) — Transaction history
    
-   Fully typed (TypeScript interfaces for all responses)
    

Stellar Usage:

-   Designed to integrate with Stellar Wallets Kit
    
-   Works with Freighter wallet signing flow
    
-   RESTful client for Soroban relayer endpoints
    

Impact: 3-5 line integration for any dApp. Developers can add C-address support without learning Soroban transaction building.

-  

5. Reference Wallet (Web)
------------------------------

What: React-based reference implementation showing C-address wallet at Freighter feature parity. Demonstrates the complete onboarding flow from wallet connection to token transfer.

Components:

-   SmartAccountPage — Main wallet view
    
-   CreateSmartAccount — One-click C-address creation
    
-   SmartAccountBalance — Multi-token display (XLM ✦, USDC $, EURC €)
    
-   SmartAccountSend — Send to G or C address, multi-asset
    
-   SmartAccountHistory — Transfer history with explorer links
    

Stellar Usage:

-   Stellar Wallets Kit for wallet connection (Freighter, Lobstr, xBull)
    
-   Freighter for transaction signing
    
-   Stellar Expert links for transaction verification
    

Impact: Serves as the reference implementation that any wallet provider can use to add C-address support. Open source, well-documented, component-based.

-  

6. Onboarding Kit
----------------------

What: The standard UX flow for C-address onboarding, packaged as reusable components:

1.  Connect — Stellar Wallets Kit integration
    
1.  Create — One-click SmartAccount deployment
    
1.  Fund — G→C bridge transfer (gasless)
    
1.  Send — C→any transfer (gasless)
    
1.  History — View all transactions
    

Stellar Usage:

-   Built on top of Stellar Wallets Kit
    
-   All flows gasless via fee-bump relayer
    
-   Multi-token (any SAC asset)
    

Impact: Provides the "standard user experience flow for wallet providers" called for in the RFP. Any wallet can integrate this kit to support C-addresses.

7. Integration Test Suite
------------------------------

What: 23 automated integration tests covering all C-address endpoints, input validation, legacy path compatibility, and Stellar relayer health.

Stellar Usage:

-   Tests against live Soroban testnet
    
-   Validates real contract interactions (lookup, balance queries)

Impact: Ensures reliability and provides a testing reference for ecosystem developers.

Products & Services:
Sorobanhooks – Product & Service Proposal

Real-Time On-Chain Data Made Simple — App-ready data instantly for developers & communities.

Sorobanhooks is a production-ready data infrastructure layer on Stellar/Soroban that transforms raw blockchain data into structured, real-time, developer-friendly APIs and human-readable insights.

Existing Live Infrastructure

We already operate live services including:

1.  Contract Alerts – Real-time Soroban contract events via Webhook, Telegram, Discord + historical contract APIs.
    
1.  Asset Alerts – Real-time price change alerts + historical pricing endpoints.
    
1.  Wallet Alerts – Instant balance & transaction notifications.
    
1.  Wallet Streams – Backendless balance-change streams for dApps.
    
1.  Cross-Chain Bridge Monitor – Cross-chain validation (Allbridge integrated).
    
1.  Contract Deployer – GitHub-to-Soroban contract deployment without local setup.
    
1.  Smart Monitoring – Converts raw on-chain data into structured, human-readable feeds.
    
1.  Smart Analysis – AI-generated daily/weekly/monthly activity summaries.
    

This demonstrates that we already operate:

-   Real-time indexing systems
    
-   Historical data storage infrastructure
    
-   High-availability APIs
    
-   Developer SDK tooling
    
-   Production alert systems
    

The proposed RFP work is a natural expansion of our current architecture — not a new experimental direction.

**Expansion Aligned with SCF RFP Tracks

1. Prices API (** https://stellar.gitbook.io/scf-handbook/scf-awards/build-award/rfp-track#prices-api )

We will develop a publicly accessible, production-grade normalized price aggregation API covering:

-   All Stellar native assets
    
-   Classic Stellar assets
    
-   SEP-41 Soroban tokens
    

Core Capabilities

-   Oracle integrations (Chainlink, Redstone, Band, Reflector, others)
    
-   On-chain DEX aggregation (Soroswap, Aquarius, SDEX, Blend pools)
    
-   VWAP calculation with configurable USD volume thresholds
    
-   Real-time price endpoints
    
-   Historical OHLC (1m to 1M granularities)
    
-   Long-term 1h+ granularity retention
    
-   Base/quote USD volume reporting
    

Built on our existing indexing pipeline, ensuring:

-   High availability
    
-   <400ms target latency
    
-   Divergence detection & fallback handling
    

2. DeFi Positions API ( https://stellar.gitbook.io/scf-handbook/scf-awards/build-award/rfp-track#defi-positions-api )

We will build a normalized DeFi exposure aggregation service supporting:

Initial protocols:

-   Blend
    
-   Aquarius
    
-   Soroswap
    
-   FxDAO
    

Output per G or C address:

-   Total current value
    
-   Deposit value
    
-   Borrow value
    
-   Current returns
    
-   Health factor
    
-   Optional historical position tracking
    

We will implement protocol-specific logic (e.g., Blend backstop pools, LP token valuation, collateral modeling) to ensure semantic accuracy.

The API will enable:

-   Portfolio dashboards
    
-   Risk analytics tools
    
-   DeFi monitoring products
    
-   Institutional reporting layers
    

3. Soroban-First Block Explorer ( https://stellar.gitbook.io/scf-handbook/scf-awards/build-award/rfp-track#soroban-first-block-explorer)

We will deliver a Soroban-native explorer with:

-   Full SEP-41 & SEP-50 support
    
-   CAP-67 unified event handling
    
-   Human-readable smart contract call decoding
    
-   Function call filtering
    
-   Swap detection heuristics
    
-   Support for Classic + Soroban operations
    
-   Protocol v20+ compatibility
    
-   Complete Soroban transaction history from 2024
    

The explorer backend will reuse our real-time indexing system and expose an open-source API layer for ecosystem builders.

Why Sorobanhooks Is the Right Team

1.  Proven Real-Time Data Infrastructure : We already index Soroban contracts, wallet events, and asset prices in production.
    
1.  Developer-Focused Tooling Experience : Our platform serves developers via APIs, webhooks, SDKs, and no-code dashboards.
    
1.  Experience Normalizing Complex On-Chain Data : We already convert raw blockchain events into structured, human-readable formats and aggregated feeds.
    
1.  Production-Ready Architecture : High-availability APIs, scalable indexing systems, alert pipelines, and historical storage are already operational.
    
1.  Ecosystem Alignment : Our mission directly supports:
    

-   Faster builder onboarding
    
-   Better DeFi UX
    
-   Improved transparency
    
-   Lower infrastructure friction  
    

Strategic Alignment with RFP

The RFP tracks (Prices API, DeFi Positions API, Soroban-first Block Explorer) are fundamentally data normalization and indexing challenges. Sorobanhooks is already a data-layer platform. This expansion strengthens Stellar’s developer infrastructure and removes fragmentation in price data, DeFi exposure tracking, and Soroban transaction visibility. We are not proposing new capabilities outside our domain — we are scaling our existing expertise to serve critical ecosystem needs.
